data.title: Costco Stock Forecast: Can COST Keep Up amid Worries about the Future of Retail Stores?
data.description:
Shares in Costco (COST), the membership-based wholesale retailer, still hold about 11% upside potential even as worries about the future of retail stores from e-commerce disruption persist.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bernstein Holds 'High Conviction' in Costco

Recently, Bernstein analyst Zhihan Ma affirmed her "high conviction in Costco's earnings power over time," noting that membership-based warehouse clubs are the best protected from disruptive forces that could sweep through retailers over the next decade.

Ma believes that the "treasure hunt" experience — or the practice of browsing aisles and finding surprising bargains — is difficult to replicate online. Furthermore, the analyst contended that Costco could bank on growth from expanding into several international markets where the presence of e-commerce remains low.

This comes as Costco grew its sales by 9.2% year-over-year during the three-month period ending on February 15. Its revenue hit $69.6 billion during the quarter.

Pitting Costco Against Walmart and Target

Nonetheless, Walmart (WMT) remains a top challenger to Costco, with Bernstein pointing to upsides from the retail giant's early mover status in building out its capabilities across multiple sales channels, such as physical stores, websites, and mobile apps. This makes Walmart "the most future-proof" of mass retailers regularly reviewed by the company.

On the contrary, Bernstein sees Target (TGT) in a "structurally challenged position" and as "the most at risk to start to close stores in the next decade." The equity research firm cited the retailer's failure to invest in automated fulfillment centers like Walmart.

It also pointed to pressure on the retailer's profit margin as core customers shift towards online shopping.

Moreover, Bernstein believes that even if Target — which recently made changes to its board to support the company's strategy — doubles down on such investments, it may not reap the same benefit as Walmart due to the smaller scale of its operations.

Is Costco Still a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, analysts continue to hold a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Costco's shares. This breaks down to 17 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell issued by 23 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average COST price target of $1,088.65 implies over 11% upside.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: SharePoint Proves an Attack Vector: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Slips
data.description:
Security issues are nothing new to tech giant Microsoft (MSFT). Since it is one of the primary sources from which people generate content, it is also one of the most ready sources of information. New reports say that Microsoft's SharePoint system is also proving to be a weapon in hackers' hands. This did not sit well with shareholders, who sent shares down modestly in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The reports note that a critical vulnerability in SharePoint that Microsoft had patched back in January is actually now potentially useful to staging attacks. The flaw in question was found in SharePoint Server Subscription Edition, SharePoint Server 2019, and SharePoint Enterprise Server 2016. Interestingly, several other SharePoint editions are currently at end-of-support status, so they are not receiving security updates, yet are still vulnerable.

Using the attack gives "threat actors" the ability to use remote code execution on servers without patches that "…exploit a deserialization of untrusted data weakness." The weakness has not yet been exploited in the field, reports note, but the CISA has added the flaw to a file of actively exploited vulnerabilities regardless.

A Japanese Expansion

Microsoft's Xbox, meanwhile, has not always done well in Japan. In a bid to stop leaving so much money on the table, Microsoft is planning to hire a regional director for its ID@XBOX platform in Japan. Reports suggest that Microsoft's game console is actually starting to catch on in Japan, and this is a sign that Microsoft may want to keep the momentum going.

Xbox has landed some of the biggest titles in the Japanese sphere, but there are plenty of indie titles that are going unnoticed, and not showing up on Xbox. Since the ID@XBOX platform is heavily focused on indie titles, this new position will help shore up some of those losses and bring more games to Xbox.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 33 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.28% rally in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $591.56 per share implies 52.46% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: $80 Billion Down the Drain as META Shuts Down Failed VR Metaverse
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is retreating from its metaverse efforts after investing heavily in its Reality Labs division, which has accumulated more than $80 billion in losses since the company began exploring virtual and augmented reality technologies. This move signals a broader strategic shift, as Meta is now focusing on artificial intelligence, while prioritizing areas with clearer revenue potential and stronger user engagement.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Scales Back Metaverse Ambitions After Heavy Losses

Meta's Reality Labs division has continued to record substantial losses since 2020, exceeding $80 billion as the company has invested in virtual and augmented reality technologies. The metaverse project was introduced, originally as a long-term vision for real digital interaction, but it has failed to gain widespread adoption and deliver consistent returns.

The losses incurred by Reality Labs have become a major concern among investors about the efficiency of Meta's capital allocation. Despite continued development in virtual reality hardware and software, the segment has remained a significant cost center, contributing to the company's decision to reassess the scale of its metaverse efforts.

Resources Redirected Toward AI and Core Operations

As a result of the losses incurred, Meta is shifting its focus and reallocating resources toward artificial intelligence, in addition to its core advertising and social media platforms. These segments continue to signify the company's primary revenue drivers and are supported by large global user bases across its family of apps.

The strategic shift shows the company is prioritizing areas with stronger monetization potential and more immediate financial returns. By focusing on AI technology and its established business lines, Meta aims to improve efficiency while concentrating on products and services that have contributed most to its overall performance in the past.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Meta Platforms remains supported by its strong digital advertising business, despite rising costs and capital expenditures. Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks rate the AI stock a 'Strong Buy,' projecting a major 42% upside. To see how other top AI and semiconductor stocks may perform amid tightening memory supply, investors can compare forecasts using the TipRanks Stock Comparison tool.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: S&P 500 Price Target Slashed to 7,200 by JPMorgan on Recession Risks
data.description:
JPMorgan lowered its 2026 year-end S&P 500 (SPX) price target to 7,200 from 7,500 on Thursday, warning that rising oil and gas prices could depress consumer demand. Head of Global Markets Strategy Dubravko Lakos-Bujas noted that further selling pressure could send the benchmark index down to 6,000-6,200 and that the U.S.-Iran war and closure of the Strait of Hormuz could last longer than many expect.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The firm now has the second-lowest price target on Wall Street, only trailing Bank of America's forecast of 7,100. The average price target sits at 7,600.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Oil Shocks Signal Recession Risk

Lakos-Bujas pointed out that oil spikes of 30% and greater have historically led to demand destruction and preceded recessions. Demand destruction from four of the five oil shocks since the 1970s preceded recessions.

The firm's price target still implies upside of 9.5% from current levels. While Lakos-Bujas has warned of near-term downside, he still expects the index to rally later this year, although the gains may be limited by geopolitical tensions.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Microsoft or CoreWeave: Billionaire Philippe Laffont Snaps Up One AI Stock and Dumps the Other
data.description:
Getting in early on breakthrough technologies has long been one of the most reliable ways to generate outsized returns in the stock market, and Philippe Laffont has built his reputation on doing exactly that.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As the founder of Coatue Management, which oversees about $54 billion in assets, Laffont was among the early backers of several prominent tech names, including TikTok parent company ByteDance, Spotify, and Snap, among others.

With that kind of track record, it's hardly surprising that the billionaire investor – whose net worth is estimated at $7.9 billion – has leaned heavily into AI, adding several leading AI names to his portfolio. Yet, alongside that enthusiasm, Laffont has also been increasingly vocal about the tensions and trade-offs embedded in this transformative technology, suggesting the opportunity may not come without meaningful risks.

"If AI becomes so successful, you could imagine the economy growing very fast, but with a lot of job destruction along the way," the billionaire recently said. "And it's sort of odd to be saying, 'Listen, we love AI, we love everything it can do, but does it also lead to a lot of unemployment?' So that's maybe more of a 10- or 15-year concern, but I sort of think about it already a little bit now."

That's not the only thing he appears to have been dwelling on. Laffont has obviously also been thinking about the prospects of the AI stocks in his portfolio. Both Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) have been among Coatue's holdings, but that is no longer the case, given the firm recently loaded up on one and exited its position in the other.

So, let's give the pair a closer look, and with some help from the TipRanks database, we can see whether his choices sit well with the Street's analyst community.

Microsoft

First up, Microsoft, a company that has had a curious AI trajectory so far. Microsoft was one of the first major tech giants to make a big strategic bet on AI by backing OpenAI, initially investing over $13 billion and becoming its exclusive cloud provider. That early support helped OpenAI scale tools like ChatGPT and integrate them into Microsoft's ecosystem, including the Azure OpenAI Service and Microsoft 365 Copilot.

That early lead, however, has been somewhat eroded as competition has intensified, with rivals like Google pushing their own AI capabilities. Microsoft continues to benefit from long-standing enterprise relationships and seamless integration across its software ecosystem, yet at the same time, questions have emerged over whether its AI-related investments will pay off. For instance, despite posting strong December quarter earnings, the stock subsequently experienced its largest drop since Covid, driven by a sharp increase in data center spending. It should also be noted that nearly half of Microsoft's $625 billion backlog is tied to OpenAI.

Given the above, a shift in sentiment has taken place. From being viewed as an early AI winner, investors have cooled on this name. To wit, shares are down by 19% year-to-date and have stayed flat over the past year.

However, it would probably be foolish to write off the tech giant, and Laffont clearly believes the company has plenty to offer in the new AI-driven paradigm. During Q4, Laffont's firm, Coatue, added 528,926 shares, a stake now worth more than $205 million.

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill understands why Philippe Laffont is betting big, pointing to the factors that position MSFT to emerge as an AI winner after all.

"We believe MSFT is structurally positioned to capture share in the agentic AI era, regardless of which foundational model leads. Importantly, MSFT is model-agnostic, and its strategy does not depend on owning the best model but on controlling the platform where models are deployed, governed, and monetized. With direct platform control of core productivity interfaces with over 450M paid M365 users, MSFT provides a full-stack AI solution with governance that CIOs trust. As value shifts to the app layer, where agents operate across applications, MSFT is positioned to monetize faster as agent-driven API usage expands the opportunity well beyond traditional human interactions," Thill opined.

Trade CRWV with leverage

To this end, Thill rates the shares as Buy while his $675 price target offers one-year upside of 73%. (To watch Thill's track record, click here)

Most other analysts are also on board here; based on a mix of 33 Buys vs. 3 Holds, the MSFT stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. Going by the $591.56 average price target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a 52% premium. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: AMD Stock Rises on a Counterfeit Ryzen Rebuttal
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock was up on Thursday after the semiconductor company released a statement tied to counterfeit labels on CHUWI laptops. According to AMD, it wasn't involved in the marketing or labeling of CHUWI laptops that erroneously claimed to have Ryzen 5 7430U CPUs but were actually equipped with Ryzen 5 5500U processors.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AMD made clear that it had no knowledge of this issue. It also suggested that it might pursue legal action against CHUWI for misrepresenting its products. It's worth noting that the mistake was more than just a simple labeling error. The affected laptops also had their system-level information altered to show them as devices equipped with Ryzen 5 7430U CPUs. This suggests the deception was intentional and not a mistake.

Here's a portion of what AMD said in its statement about the CHUWI laptops: "AMD has always placed great importance on the authenticity and transparency of product information, and is committed to maintaining a fair and orderly market environment and protecting the legitimate rights and interests of users. Our company has taken this matter very seriously and reserves the right to pursue legal action against the relevant parties."

AMD Stock Jumps Today

AMD stock was up 0.43% on Thursday but was still down 6.39% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 86.17% over the past 12 months.

AMD trading activity today was low, with some 15 million shares traded, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 38.13 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: PayPal (PYPL) Investors Risk Losing Spot in Class Action Lawsuit if They Miss This Deadline
data.description:
PayPal (PYPL) investors have a fast-approaching deadline to file for lead plaintiff status in the securities fraud class action lawsuit against the company. The suit follows a series of events that have shaken investor sentiment, sending PYPL stock down more than 23% year-to-date (YTD), according to Tipranks data. 

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Deadline to Join PayPal's Securities Fraud Lawsuit

According to official reports, PayPal investors have until April 20, 2026, to file for lead plaintiff status in the ongoing lawsuit. The case, brought to investors' attention by Pennsylvania-based securities litigation firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (KTMC), covers those who purchased PayPal common stock between Feb. 25, 2025, and Feb. 2, 2026.

The lawsuit alleges that PayPal misled investors about its revenue outlook and growth prospects while downplaying risks from seasonality and macroeconomic factors. It further claims that the company's plans to expand PayPal's Branded Checkout offerings and meet 2027 targets were unrealistic and required both a stable, unrealistic consumer environment and flawless execution. KTMC is urging affected investors to contact attorney Jonathan Naji for a free evaluation, with representation available on a contingency-fee basis at no upfront cost.

The Events Behind PayPal's Lawsuit

On Feb. 3, 2026, PayPal reported Q4 revenue of $8.68 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.23 per share, missing consensus estimates of $8.80 billion and $1.28, respectively, according to LSEG data. Following this, the company withdrew its 2027 targets and issued a softer 2026 profit outlook, sending shares to nine-year lows.

The report also revealed that PayPal's Branded Checkout growth slowed to 1%, down from 6% a year earlier, largely due to weakness in U.S. retail. The results capped a difficult tenure for outgoing CEO Alex Chriss, who had aimed to expand PayPal's Branded Checkout business amid slowing post-pandemic volumes and intensifying competition from fintech rivals including Apple Pay (AAPL), Klarna (KLAR), and Stripe. The leadership change raised fresh concerns about the company's strategic direction amid its ongoing lawsuit and mounting competitive pressures.

Is PayPal a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

TipRanks forecast data shows PayPal (PYPL) holds a consensus "Hold" rating based on 34 recent analyst reviews, including 5 Buy, 24 Hold, and 4 Sell recommendations. The average 12-month price target stands at $50.71, implying a modest 9% upside from current levels. For investors searching for other top payment stocks, visit TipRanks Stock Comparison Center for analysts' updates, price targets, performance metrics, and more.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: 'Innovation Not Price Cuts' Chinese Government Tells Discounting Domestic EV Makers
data.description:
Chinese EV makers such as BYD (BYDDY) and XPeng (XPEV) have been warned by their government to focus on innovation and not price cuts to keep the sector healthy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EV Makers Meeting

According to a report in the South China Morning Post, a high level meeting of the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, the National Development and Reform Commission and the State Administration for Market Regulation, gathered 17 major carmakers to "further regulate the competition order" in the sector.  In short, it was a call to move away from aggressive discounting and to focus instead on technological innovation, such as chips and self-driving systems, as the best form of competition.

It comes as domestic manufacturers try and tackle cooling consumer demand and the phasing out of long-standing government subsidies, by cutting prices and offering incentives such as zero-interest financing.

It is a tactic which doesn't always pay dividends. February data from China showed that BYD's sales dropped to 190,190 vehicles, down 41.1% year over year and 9.5% sequentially, while rival maker Geely's (GELYF) combined brands delivered 206,000 vehicles and held the top position for a second month.

A Volatile Market

Analysts said this showed that China's EV market remains highly competitive and volatile, with leadership changing quickly despite BYD having closed 2025 with record performance. It could, they said, intensify market scrutiny of BYD's demand sustainability, incentive structures, and the impact of ongoing price competition.

Indeed, the government believes this "price war" is seen as "destructive competition" that could destabilize the industry and squeeze profits. It could also, it fears, lead to deflationary pressures and hurt the sector's supply chain through issues such as delayed payments to suppliers.

It worries that some EVs could even go bust if the situation continues.

What are the Best EV Stocks to Buy Now?

We have rounded up the best EV stocks to buy now using our TipRanks Comparison Tool.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Retreats as Oil Climbs Higher; Iran Targets Energy Infrastructure
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading lower on Thursday as investors digest escalating tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Iran vowed to retaliate by targeting key oil infrastructure sites in the Middle East after Israel launched missiles on its South Pars gas field. Over the past 24 hours, Iran has carried out aerial attacks on Saudi Aramco's Samref refinery, Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation's Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries. Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, are up by over 2% as the Strait of Hormuz remains at a near standstill.

To counter higher prices, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the U.S. is considering lifting sanctions on Iranian oil and a second Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) release. Removing the sanctions would allow oil to "flow into Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, Japan, India — who have been good actors in this," he said.

Furthermore, President Trump is considering sending ground troops to Iran, according to Reuters. The troops could provide security to tankers crossing through the Strait of Hormuz or conduct an operation to secure Iran's stockpile of highly ​enriched uranium.

Elsewhere, the labor market remains in a slow-to-fire-and-hire environment. Initial jobless claims for the week ended March 14 fell by 8,000 to 205,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, rose by 10,000 to 1.857 million and were slightly above the estimate of 1.852 million. Initial jobless claims indicate recent layoffs, while continuing claims measure the number of people still receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

The Dow Jones is down by 0.73% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan
